description_long: This all-terrain vehicle is a compact, six-wheeled robotic rover with a robust, utilitarian design reminiscent of NASA's Mars Exploration Rovers. It features a central body with four large, flat, black solar panel arrays extending outward, providing both power and a protective shell. The vehicle is equipped with a prominent mast topped with a sensor or camera array, and a dish antenna is mounted on one side for communication. Its wheels are rugged and widely spaced, designed for stability and traction on uneven surfaces, and the suspension system appears articulated for navigating rough terrain. The chassis is constructed from a combination of metallic and composite materials, with visible joints, actuators, and exposed wiring, emphasizing its functional, exploratory purpose. The overall appearance is high-tech and engineered for durability in challenging environments.
description: A six-wheeled, solar-powered robotic rover with a central mast, dish antenna, and articulated suspension, designed for rugged all-terrain exploration. Its body is covered with black solar panels and metallic components, giving it a high-tech, durable look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central mast with sensor array, symmetrical solar panels, and two large front wheels.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the articulated suspension, three visible wheels, and the dish antenna on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the mast, solar panels, and the rear wheel arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side of the vehicle, with the mast and suspension system visible.
attribute_tire_tread_pattern: The tires have a blocky, all-terrain tread pattern with deep grooves, designed for optimal traction on loose or uneven surfaces.
